text:
Taylor Fritzis set to play three matches at theOlympicson Wednesday with the schedule having thrown up a gruelling run of fixtures. The 26-year-old is representing the USA in three events after being named in their squad for the men's singles, men's doubles and mixed doubles.

Fritz, will face Lorenzo Musetti in the third round of the men's singles after beating Alexander Bublik and Jack Draper in his first two matches. In the men's doubles, he will partner Tommy Paul against the Dutch pairing of Robin Haase and Jean-Julien Rojer in the second round.

He will also be in action with Coco Gauff in the quarter-finals of the mixed doubles against Argentina duo Maximo Gonzalez and Nadia Podoroska. There is just one problem, though. All three of those matches are scheduled to take place within hours of each other on the same day.



Our community members are treated to special offers, promotions, and adverts from us and our partners. You can check out at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

The singles match between Fritz and Musetti is scheduled to be first up on Court 14 at Roland Garros. Fritz will return to the same court for the third match of the day in the men's doubles before heading to Court Mathieu to play in the last eight of the mixed doubles.

He will only have a few hours to recover before playing three matches in quick succession after securing a comeback victory over Draper in the men's singles on Tuesday. He dropped the first set but managed to keep his composure, battling hard in scorching conditions to win the next two and seal the match.

Don't miss:Murray delays retirement again as Brit reaches Olympics quarter-final with Evans

Don't miss:Calls to 'protect' Emma Raducanu ramp up as Brit sent warning over coaches

Don't miss:Show your colours and get Team GB's official kit for the Paris Olympics

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

The heat in Paris has soared to 35 degrees Celsius at times, with Olympic bosses giving players a 10-minute break between the second and third sets. Speaking after his defeat to Fritz,Draper branded the conditions 'brutal' and complained that he was forced to drink warm waterwith no chilled bottles on hand.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

He explained: "I said to the referee: 'It’s pretty poor that the players have to drink hot water when we’re playing in those conditions'. I don’t know how hot it was but it definitely felt very, very difficult. I haven’t played in much hotter conditions than that.

"Your body starts to really fight against you. I definitely feel like he [Fritz] dealt with it better today."

title: Olympics tennis star to play three matches in one day in nightmare schedule | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Archie Griggs
description: Taylor Fritz is set for a hectic day at the Olympics after being handed a nightmare schedule.
